description_long: This object is a traditional hand axe featuring a robust wooden handle and a single, broad metal blade. The handle is cylindrical, straight, and appears to be made from a medium-brown hardwood, showing subtle grain patterns and a smooth finish. The blade is mounted perpendicularly to the handle, with a wide, slightly curved cutting edge and a flat poll opposite the edge. The blade is matte black, likely forged steel, and is securely fastened to the handle with a classic collar or socket design. There are no visible decorative elements or branding, giving the axe a utilitarian and functional appearance. The overall proportions suggest it is designed for one-handed use, suitable for chopping wood or similar tasks in an urban or residential setting.
description: A traditional hand axe with a straight wooden handle and a broad, matte black metal blade, designed for practical chopping tasks. The design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible markings or decorations.
description_view_0: The axe is seen from the top, showing the pointed end of the blade and the cylindrical handle extending downward.
description_view_1: The axe is viewed from the side, clearly displaying the wide, curved blade and the full length of the wooden handle.
description_view_2: The axe is seen from the back, showing the flat poll of the blade and the straight handle.
description_view_3: The opposite side view of the axe, again highlighting the broad blade and the wooden handle, mirroring view 1.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical wooden handle, smooth finish, no visible grip features, round cross-section.
attribute_blade_design: Single broad steel blade with a slightly curved edge, flat poll, no beard, matte black finish, utilitarian design.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged or socket mounting, blade fitted perpendicularly to handle.
